name: typescript
summary: TypeScript is a language for application scale JavaScript development

story.problem: JavaScript is dynamically typed — typos in property names, passing the wrong type to a function, and refactoring without knowing all callers are all silent runtime errors. In large codebases, these become expensive bugs. TypeScript adds a static type system that catches them at compile time without changing how the code runs.
story.mental_model: TypeScript is a strict superset of JavaScript: all valid JavaScript is valid TypeScript. You add type annotations where they help and TypeScript infers the rest. The compiler strips all types before running, so the output is plain JavaScript. Think of types as documentation that the computer checks.
story.when_to_use: Use TypeScript for any JavaScript project that will grow beyond a few hundred lines or be worked on by more than one person. The upfront cost of adding types pays back quickly in editor autocomplete, safe refactoring, and catching bugs before runtime.
story.when_not_to_use: TypeScript adds a compilation step and a learning curve. For tiny scripts, config files, or throwaway code, plain JavaScript is faster to write. TypeScript's type system also has limits — it can't track runtime behaviour, only the shape of values at compile time.

alternatives.name: jsdoc
alternatives.reason: Type comments in JavaScript without a build step — TypeScript can even check these; good for small projects

alternatives.name: flow
alternatives.reason: Meta's type checker; similar to TypeScript but smaller ecosystem and community

nodes.label: typescript (compiler API)
nodes.summary: The TypeScript compiler package. CLI: `tsc`. Programmatic API: `import ts from 'typescript'`.

nodes.label: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Project config: compilerOptions (target, module, strict, paths, baseUrl), include, exclude.

nodes.label: Type System Primitives
nodes.summary: string, number, boolean, null, undefined, symbol, bigint, unknown, never, any, void.

nodes.label: Generics
nodes.summary: Parameterized types: `function identity<T>(x: T): T`. Constraints with extends. Default type params.

nodes.label: Utility Types
nodes.summary:
Partial, Required, Readonly, Pick, Omit, Record, ReturnType, Awaited

.

nodes.label: Union & Intersection Types
nodes.summary: A | B (either), A & B (both). Discriminated unions with literal type tags.

nodes.label: Mapped & Conditional Types
nodes.summary: { [K in keyof T]: ... }. T extends U ? X : Y. infer keyword for type extraction.

nodes.label: Decorators
nodes.summary: Experimental metadata annotations for classes, methods, properties. Enable with experimentalDecorators.

nodes.label: Compiler API (ts.*)
nodes.summary: ts.createProgram(), ts.createSourceFile(), AST traversal with ts.forEachChild(), ts.Diagnostic.

nodes.label: Declaration Files (.d.ts)
nodes.summary: Type-only files for JS libraries. Managed by @types/* on npm or authored manually.
